response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, distributed systems, and geospatial data collection, but the role requires deep, current C++ or Rust expertise for a production routing tile pipeline — which his profile does not provide. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; it does not appear in his skills section and has not been a focus for over a decade. His geospatial work (GISCollective, Green Map) is relevant in domain but centers on data collection and visualization platforms, not routing tile generation or navigation pipeline optimization. His primary stack is firmly TypeScript/Node.js, making him a weak technical match for this position.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain experience — built GIS platforms, gave a FOSS4G talk, and maintained map-data tooling across multiple roles
Demonstrated distributed-systems exposure — low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, craftsmanship orientation evident across roles
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from his current skill set; last meaningful use was 10+ years ago and not listed in his skills section
No Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
No routing pipeline, navigation guidance, or routing-tile-specific experience — the geospatial work is data collection, not routing optimization
